verification_gate: The matrix covers every recorded state family exactly once, resolves all overlaps and gaps, makes each synchronization edge directional and idempotent, names identity and recovery authorities, contains no unresolved dual-write path, and keeps every execution target Draft while the global graph gate is closed.

clause_1_covers_every_recorded_state_fam.single_assignment_check: 13 unique work-item ids; the 'durable' closure family splits into the two owners STORE-001 names separately (M1 relational, M2 semantic); the transient family expands into the seven individually-resolved PGREDB rows so no redb use is double-counted or dropped

clause_2_resolves_all_overlaps_and_gaps.central_contradiction_PGREDB_006: The source's transient-buffer framing vs CodeDB's verified durable-default is resolved (PGAUTH-006-R6) by binding the row to the R1 verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover: redb's transient-forward-buffer role is realized only as the durable class migrates to PostgreSQL under parity, never by assumption
clause_2_resolves_all_overlaps_and_gaps.durable_today_PGREDB_001: Decided as TRANSITIONAL (PGAUTH-006-R1): target owner PostgreSQL via codedb_store_pg::PgStore under one SHA-256 identity; not silently reclassified
clause_2_resolves_all_overlaps_and_gaps.durable_today_PGREDB_002: Decided (PGAUTH-006-R2): transient runtime store + one-way promotion of settled audit history to PostgreSQL; preserved, not erased
clause_2_resolves_all_overlaps_and_gaps.durable_split: 'durable' overlap between relational and semantic resolved by assigning both to PostgreSQL+RuVector with the semantic index explicitly derived from versioned source rows (M2), never an independent authority
clause_2_resolves_all_overlaps_and_gaps.gap_none_remaining: All five parent contracts (PGAUTH-001..005) and all seven PGREDB rows are assigned; no 'pending STORE-001' cell remains open

clause_3_each_synchronization_edge_direc.directional: Every edge is forward-only: redb->envctl->PostgreSQL (M1/M3/R1..R6), local-embed->PostgreSQL (M2), AgentDB->PostgreSQL one-way promotion (M4), PostgreSQL->envctl->filesystem (M5/R7), external->inbound ingestion (M6)

idempotency_mechanisms.R4: BLAKE3(content+model+version+config) key; deterministic recompute on miss
idempotency_mechanisms.R5: monotonic sequence + operation idempotency key = at-most-once apply

recovery_authorities.transient: redb local disk (process-lifecycle); disposable classes recompute; outbox replays to PostgreSQL
recovery_authorities.cognitive: AgentDB .rvf file; regenerable from base model + training history
recovery_authorities.external: OS keyring + committed pins; fail-closed re-solicit/re-retrieve
recovery_authorities.codedb_blobs: redb file + working tree today; PostgreSQL PITR after verified cutover
recovery_authorities.envctl_migration: redb file + envctl replay engine (runtime); PostgreSQL PITR (promoted audit)

clause_5_no_unresolved_dual_write_path.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL+RuVector owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class (STORE-001 invariant 1)
clause_5_no_unresolved_dual_write_path.subordinates_named: outbox (R5) and CodeDB blob store (R1/R6) are subordinate/transitional to PostgreSQL; envctl (R2) is not the synchronizing durable writer of macro-history (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted); the UI shared-redb view (R7) is read-only and never a dual-writer
clause_5_no_unresolved_dual_write_path.verified_cutover_guard: a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL; never by assumption
clause_5_no_unresolved_dual_write_path.contested_row_closed: PGREDB-006 is no longer an unresolved dual-write path; it is bound to the R1 verified cutover

clause_6_every_execution_target_stays_dr.planning_only: This task produced planning artifacts (task families + matrix + proof); no live database, schema, or runtime state was created or mutated

store_001_alignment.assignment_map: M1<-assignment 1 (durable relational -> PostgreSQL), M2<-assignment 2 (semantic/vector -> PostgreSQL+RuVector No-Sidecar), M3<-assignment 3 (transient -> redb), M4<-assignment 4 (per-agent cognition -> AgentDB), M5<-assignment 5 (derived projections -> PostgreSQL/envctl), M6<-assignment 6 (external-secret -> keyring+pins)
store_001_alignment.upgrades_not_downgrades: Honored by the migration MECHANISM (SHA-256 BlobStore parity), not by freezing redb as durable; PGREDB-001/002/006 durable uses are preserved via verified cutover / forward promotion, not erased

proof_summary: PGAUTH-006 closes the state-ownership synchronization and recovery matrix by reconciling the five parent authority contracts (PGAUTH-001 durable, PGAUTH-002 redb inventory, PGAUTH-003 cognitive, PGAUTH-004 projected, PGAUTH-005 external) and the seven concrete redb usage rows (PGREDB-001..007) into 13 single-assignment rows aligned to STORE-001's six canonical classes. The six macro classes assign durable relational macro-state and semantic/vector state to PostgreSQL+RuVector (M1, M2 native No-Sidecar), the transient high-frequency tier to redb (M3), per-agent cognition to AgentDB .rvf (M4), derived projections to PostgreSQL source projected by envctl (M5), and external/secret authority to the OS keyring plus committed pins (M6). The seven redb rows are each decided rather than erased: CodeDB source_blobs (R1/PGREDB-001, durable today) migrate to PostgreSQL-canonical via the already-implemented codedb_store_pg::PgStore under one SHA-256 identity; envctl migration_db (R2/PGREDB-002, durable today) is a transient runtime store whose settled audit history promotes one-way to PostgreSQL; agent scratchpad (R3), embedding cache (R4, derived from M2 via BLAKE3), and the application-level WAL/outbox (R5, subordinate to PostgreSQL via monotonic sequence + idempotency key) stay transient in redb; the CONTESTED ingestion-buffer row (R6/PGREDB-006) is resolved by binding it to the R1 verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover so redb's transient-buffer role is realized only as the durable class moves to PostgreSQL, never by assumption; and the shared-redb UI view (R7/PGREDB-007) is a read-only projection over the transient agent tier, never a second writable authority. Every synchronization edge is forward-only and made idempotent by SHA-256, BLAKE3, SHAKE256 witness chains, monotonic-sequence idempotency keys, or deterministic byte-exact re-projection; every row names an identity boundary and a recovery source (PostgreSQL PITR, redb local disk, AgentDB .rvf regeneration, re-projection, keyring re-solicit); and no unresolved dual-write path remains because PostgreSQL is the single durable writer and all buffers/stores/views are explicitly subordinate, transitional, or read-only under the verified-cutover guard. All eight execution targets (POSTGRES-001/002 and PGAUTH-001..006) remain Draft in generated/task_graph.source.csv; no product execution, live database, schema, or runtime mutation was performed.

live_commands.purpose: Validate PGAUTH-006 matrix gate: exactly-once coverage (13 rows = 6 macro + 7 redb, unique ids), every row carries sync_edge/idempotency/identity_boundary/recovery_source/forbidden_dual_write, all rows closed, and all eight execution targets remain Draft
live_commands.command: python3 (matrix-gate validation over generated/task_families/PGAUTH-006.json and generated/task_graph.source.csv)
output: macro classes: 6 (M1..M6); redb rows: 7 (R1..R7); unique work-item ids: True total 13
execution target statuses all Draft: True (POSTGRES-001/002, PGAUTH-001..006)

store_001_alignment: generated/store_relationship_decision.json — matrix rows M1..M6 map one-to-one onto STORE-001 state_class_assignments 1..6; the durable/transient/cognitive/projected/external families of pgauth_closure_matrix.csv are subsumed; upgrades-not-downgrades is honored by the SHA-256 BlobStore parity migration mechanism, not by freezing redb as durable.
rollback_plan: Restore the prior task-graph revision and retain this matrix as evidence; no live state changes occurred during planning.
